environment.ckks_backend: Microsoft SEAL through TenSEAL sealapi; CPU only
environment.python: 3.11.15 | packaged by Anaconda, Inc. | (main, Mar 11 2026, 17:12:15) [MSC v.1942 64 bit (AMD64)]

scope.byte_counts: application-frame bytes; TCP/IP headers and TLS record overhead are not packet-captured
scope.framing: custom deterministic binary frame with message type, part count, uint64 part lengths, and existing serialized CKKS envelopes
scope.network: real persistent IPv4 stream sockets bound to 127.0.0.1; this is a loopback protocol/framing benchmark, not a LAN or WAN claim

startup.certificate_verification: disabled for fixed self-signed loopback benchmark certificate

methodology.level_order: ascending on even restarts and descending on odd restarts to reduce monotone thermal/order bias
methodology.oversubscription_control: each worker executes the single-threaded SEAL kernel; BLAS/OpenMP thread pools fixed to one; concurrency 16 equals logical CPU count but exceeds the 10 physical cores on this hybrid CPU
methodology.rss_scope: sampled spawned-worker RSS/working set; sums may double-count shared pages
methodology.scaling_scope: provider-side closed-loop saturation: one parent client thread and one independent public-only spawned CKKS worker per concurrency slot; query encryption and response decryption excluded from workload timer

memory.scope: sum of per-worker OS RSS/working-set samples; shared pages can be counted once per process

definition.fresh_session: new CKKS key material, serialized public context, newly spawned server, then first full client request; OS page cache is not flushed
definition.setup_timer_scope: create_context_pair includes key generation, SEAL serialization, and a local public-context deserialize/role check
definition.warm_session: subsequent full encrypt + Pipe + server + decrypt requests on the same keys and persistent server process

key_material.exact_serialization_scope: SEAL save() payloads plus packed_ckks public-context envelope
